steps.title: Combine like terms in $(4-5x)$
steps.content: Both sides contain copies of $(4-5x)$. On the left, $2(4-5x)+7(4-5x) = 9(4-5x)$, so the equation becomes $9(4-5x)+9 = 8(4-5x)+6$.

steps.title: Isolate $(4-5x)$
steps.content: Subtract $8(4-5x)$ from both sides: $(4-5x)+9 = 6$. Subtract $9$ from both sides: $(4-5x) = -3$.

steps.title: Convert to the target expression
steps.content: Since $5x-4 = -(4-5x)$, we have $5x-4 = -(-3) = 3$. The answer is 3.
